owl:Ontology.dc:creator: David Shotton
Silvio Peroni
owl:Ontology.dc:title: FaBiO, the FRBR-aligned Bibliographic Ontology
owl:Ontology.terms:bibliographicCitation: Peroni, S., Shotton, D. (2012). FaBiO and CiTO: ontologies for describing bibliographic resources and citations. In Journal of Web Semantics, 17: 33-43. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.websem.2012.08.001. Open Access at: http://speroni.web.cs.unibo.it/publications/peroni-2012-fabio-cito-ontologies.pdf
owl:Ontology.vocab:introduction:
The _FRBR-aligned Bibliographic Ontology_ (_FaBiO_) is an ontology for recording and publishing on the Semantic Web descriptions of entities that are published or potentially publishable, and that contain or are referred to by bibliographic references, or entities used to define such bibliographic references. FaBiO entities are primarily textual publications such as books, magazines, newspapers and journals, and items of their content such as poems, conference papers and editorials. However, they also include blogs, web pages, datasets, computer algorithms, experimental protocols, formal specifications and vocabularies, legal records, governmental papers, technical and commercial reports and similar publications, and also anthologies, catalogues and similar collections.
FaBiO classes are structured according to the **FRBR schema** of **Works**, **Expressions**, **Manifestations** and **Items**. Additional properties have been added to extends the FRBR data model by linking Works and Manifestations (`fabio:hasManifestation` and `fabio:isManifestationOf`), Works and Items (`fabio:hasPortrayal` and `fabio:isPortrayedBy`), and Expressions and Items (`fabio:hasRepresentation` and `fabio:isRepresentedBy`).

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: An entity primarily responsible for making the resource.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has creator
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples of a Creator include a person, an organization, or a service. Typically, the name of a Creator should be used to indicate the entity.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has format
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples of dimensions include size and duration. Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the list of Internet Media Types.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A language of the resource.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has language
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples include written, spoken, sign, and computer languages. Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as RFC 4646 [http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4646.txt].

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A legal document giving official permission to do something with the resource.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has license

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: An entity responsible for making the resource available.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has publisher
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples of a Publisher include a person, an organization, or a service. Typically, the name of a Publisher should be used to indicate the entity.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: Information about rights held in and over the resource.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has rights
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:scopeNote: Typically, rights information includes a statement about various property rights associated with the resource, including intellectual property rights.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: The discipline to which a subject vocabulary belongs.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has discipline

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property linking a particular work to its manifestations.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has manifestation
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:editorialNote: This property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: The place (usually, the city) where the publisher of a particular bibliographic resource is located.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has place of publication

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property linking a particular work to its items. This property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has portrayal

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: This property is used to associate a frbr:Endeavour to a term in a particular classification system - and the term is considered one of the main topics for the endeavour in consideration.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has primary subject term

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property linking a particular expression to its items.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has representation
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:editorialNote: This property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: This property is used to associate a `frbr:Endeavour` to a term in a particular classification system.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has subject term

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: This property relates a subject vocabulary to the discipline to which it belongs.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is discipline of

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property linking a particular manifestation to the work it is manifesting.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is manifestation of
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:editorialNote: This property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property linking a particular item to the work it portrays.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is portrayal of
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:editorialNote: This property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property linking a particular item to the expression it represents.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is representation of
owl:ObjectProperty.skos:editorialNote: This property is additional to the relationships between FRBR endeavours present in the classical FRBR data model.

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: This property expresses the fact that a scheme contains a concept.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is scheme of

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: This property relates a fabio:Item to the medium upon which it is stored.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is stored on

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: This property relates a storage medium to the `fabio:Item` stored upon it.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: stores

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property representing a manifestation that embodies an expression.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has embodiment

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property representing an expression that is embodied by a manifestation.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is embodiment of

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property representing an item that exemplifies a manifestation.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has exemplar

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property representing a manifestation that is exemplified by an item.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is exemplar of

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property indicating that one entity includes another entity.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has part

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property indicating that one entity is a part of another entity.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is part of

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property representing an expression that is an intellectual or artistic realization of a work.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: has realization

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: A property representing the work that has been realized by an expression.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is realization of

owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:comment: This property expresses the fact that a concept is a part of a scheme.
owl:ObjectProperty.rdfs:label: is in scheme

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An alternative title for a resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: alternate title

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A Digital Object Identifier for a serial publication item, a dataset or some other entity.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has DOI
has Digital Object Identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An International Standard Serial Number, ISSN, for electronic versions of serial publications.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has eISSN
has electronic International Standard Serial Number

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier for one of several alternative issues of a magazine, a book or other resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has edition

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A property used to identify the last page of an entity such as a journal article.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has ending page

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The International Standard Book Number, ISBN, used to identify published books or, more rarely, journal issues.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has ISBN
has International Standard Book Number

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The International Standard Serial Number, ISSN, an identifier for serial publications.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has ISSN
has International Standard Serial Number

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier used to identify a specific issue of a magazine, a journal or other resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has issue identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.vann:example: :my-research-paper a fabio:ResearchPaper ; prism:keyword 'legal ontologies' , 'methodology' , 'ontology modeling' , 'professional knowledge' , 'rhetorical structure theory' .
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A property used to tag a resource with a keyword that is likely to be used in search queries.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has keyword

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A property used to identify the page range of an entity such as a journal article.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has page range

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which a resource is published or disclosed.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has publication date
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: The date can be expressed in three different formats: - yyyy-mm-dd (i.e., xsd:date) - yyyy-mm (i.e., xsd:gYearMonth) - yyyy (i.e., xsd:gYear)

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The name or identifier of the section or part of a resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: section
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples include Part 2 of Issue 6 of Volume 127 of the Journal of Cell Biology, or the Travel Section of a newspaper issue.

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A property used to identify the first page of an entity such as a journal article.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has starting page

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier for a version of a resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has version identifier
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples include a text book, a draft of an article, or a data file.

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier for a particular volume of a resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has volume identifier
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples include a journal or a multi-volume book.

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The count of the number of words in a textual resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has word count

owl:DatatypeProperty.vann:example: :my-journal-article a fabio:JournalArticle ; dcterms:abstract 'This article explores the new frontiers of ontologies applied to AI in 2026.'@en .
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A summary of the resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: abstract

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which an entity has been created.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has creation date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A point or period of time associated with an event in the lifecycle of the resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has date
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: Date may be used to express temporal information at any level of granularity. Recommended best practice is to use an encoding scheme, such as the W3CDTF profile of ISO 8601 [http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime].

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date of acceptance of an entity.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has acceptance date
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples of entities to which a Date Accepted may be relevant are a thesis (accepted by a university examination board) or an article (accepted by a journal editor).

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which an entity has been copyrighted.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has copyright date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which an entity has been submitted.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has submission date
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: Examples of resources to which a Date Submitted may be relevant are a thesis (submitted to a university department), a grant application (submitted to a funding agency) or an article (submitted to a journal).

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A unique and unambiguous reference to the resource within a given context.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has identifier
owl:DatatypeProperty.skos:scopeNote: Recommended best practice is to identify the resource by means of a string conforming to a formal identification system.

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: Date of formal issuance of a resource (e.g. a publication or a patent).
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has issue date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which an entity has been modified.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has modification date

owl:DatatypeProperty.vann:example: :my-journal-article a fabio:JournalArticle ; dcterms:title 'OPJK and DILIGENT: ontology modeling in a distributed environment'@en .
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A name given to a resource. Typically, the title will be the name by which the resource is formally known.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has title

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: Date (often a range) of validity of a resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has validity date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which a particular endeavour, such as an ontology, was last updated.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: date last updated

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which a particular digital item, such as a PDF or an HTML file, has been accessed by somebody.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has access date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier used by the preprint repository ArXiv.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has ArXiv identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A CODEN is a six character, alphanumeric bibliographic identification code, that provides concise, unique and unambiguous identification of the titles of serials and non-serial publications.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has CODEN

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The count of the number of characters in a textual resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has character count

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The year in which an entity has been copyrighted.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has copyright year

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which something, for example a document, is corrected.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has correction date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which some item has been collected, for example the data gathered by means of questionnaires.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has date collected

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which some item is received, for example a document being received by a publisher.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has date received

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A date by which something has to be done.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has deadline

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which a particular endeavour, such as a grant application, has been or will be approved or rejected by somebody.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has decision date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which an entity has been deposited, for example in a library, repository, supplementary information archive, database or similar place of document or information storage.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has deposit date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which something is distributed, for example the date on which a preprint of a document is e-mailed to colleagues and other academics by the author(s), or the date on which a printed announcement of forthcoming theatre events is mailed to those those on the theatre's mailing list.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has preprint dissemination date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A local identifier for an article within an electronic (i.e. on line, in HTML format) periodical issue. Use in preference to prism:startingPage when the article lacks page numbers
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has electronic article identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date before which an entity should not be published, or before which a press release should not be reported on. For open-access journal articles, the embargo date is the date before which availability of the open-access version of the article is restricted by the publisher, following subscription-access availability of the published work. The duration of the embargo period can be specified by fabio:hasEmbargoDuration.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has embargo date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The time period for which an entity is embargoed. During this period, the entity should not be published or, in the case of a press release, should not be reported on. For open-access journal articles, the embargo duration specifies that period of time during which availability of the open-access version of the article is delayed by the publisher, following subscription-access availability of the published work. The end of the embargo period can be specified by fabio:hasEmbargoDate.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has embargo period

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A persistent identifier of the Handle system for digital objects and other resources on the Internet.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has handle

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A linking International Standard Serial Number.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has ISSN-L

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An internal identifier for the abbreviation of the title of journals available from the National Library of Medicine repository.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has National Library of Medicine journal title abbreviation

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An internal identifier for journals available from the National Library of Medicine repository.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has National Library of Medicine journal identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: Has Publisher Item Identifier
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has PII

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The count of the number of pages in a textual resource.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has page count

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A unique identifying number issued by a patent authority to identify a patent, displayed at the beginning of the patent document.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has patent number

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier for bibliographic entities hosted by the PubMed Central repository.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has PubMed Central identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier for bibliographic records held by the PubMed repository.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has PubMed identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The year in which a resource is published.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has publication year

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which an agent is requested to do something, for example a reviewer is requested to write a review of a paper submitted to a journal for publication, or an author is requested to supply a revised version of the paper in response to the reviews received.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has request date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The date on which something, for example a claim or a journal article, is retracted.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has retraction date

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The Serial Item and Contribution Identifier is a code used to uniquely identify specific volumes, articles or other identifiable parts of a periodical. It is intended primarily for use by those members of the bibliographic community involved in the use or management of serial titles and their contributions.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has SICI
has Serial Item and Contribution Identifier

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: Permits specification of the season of the year, for example spring, summer, autumn and winter in British English.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has season

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A literal (for example a number or a letter) that identifies the sequence position of a work within a particular context, for example a book in a book series, a chapter in a document, a volume in a journal.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has number

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A short version of the title of an entity, typically used to label or refer to a particular entity in an abbreviated form, for example an abbreviated journal title in a reference, or a short title of a document used as the running title in a page header.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has short title

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The number defining an international standard, for example Z39.96 - 201x, identifying NISO JATS, the Journal Article Tag Suite.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has standard number

owl:DatatypeProperty.vann:example: :my-journal-article a fabio:JournalArticle ; fabio:hasSubtitle 'Practical Guide to Ontologies and Linked Data'@en .
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A secondary title that follows the main title of a work.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has subtitle

owl:DatatypeProperty.vann:example: :my-journal-article a fabio:JournalArticle ; fabio:hasTranslatedSubtitle 'Guida pratica alle ontologie e ai Linked Data'@it .
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A version of the subtitle of an entity translated into another language, which may be specified using the object property dcterms:language.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has translated subtitle

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A version of the title of an entity translated into another language, which may be specified using the object property dcterms:language.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has translated title

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: An identifier, in form of an HTTP Universal Resource Locator (URL), for a particular resource on the World Wide Web.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has URL

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: The count of the number of volumes a work includes.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: has volume count

owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:comment: A property that identifies the calendar system used to specify a date, for example the Chinese, Gregorian, Hebrew, Islamic or Lunar calendar.
owl:DatatypeProperty.rdfs:label: uses calendar

owl:Class.vann:example: :my-abstract a fabio:Abstract ; c4o:hasContent 'In the legal domain, ontologies enjoy quite some reputation as a way to model normative knowledge about laws and jurisprudence.'@en .
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A brief summary of a work on a particular subject, designed to act as the point-of-entry that will help the reader quickly to obtain an overview of the work's contents. The abstract may be an integral part of the work itself, written by the same author(s) and appearing at the beginning of a work such as a research paper, report, review or thesis. Alternatively it may be separate from the published work itself, and written by someone other than the author(s) of the published work, for example by a member of a professional abstracting service such as CAB Abstracts.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: abstract

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document containing the programme and collected papers, or their abstracts, presented at an academic meeting.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: academic proceedings

owl:Class.vann:example: :my-addendum a fabio:Addendum ; dcterms:title 'Addendum: Unified framework for open quantum dynamics with memory'@en .
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An item of material added at the end of a book or other publication, typically to include omitted or late-arriving material.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: addendum

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A precise sequential set of pre-defined logical rules or computational operations to be employed for solving a particular problem in a finite number of steps.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: algorithm

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A real object that is an exemplar of a `fabio:Manifestation`, such as a particular copy of the book 'Alice's adventures in Wonderland', that a person may own.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: analog item
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: An analog item is an exemplar of an analog manifestation only and it is always stored in a storage medium suitable for analog objects, such as paper, vinyl discs and films.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A manifestation in an analog form.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: analog manifestation

owl:Class.vann:example: fabio:paper a fabio:AnalogStorageMedium .
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A means of storing information in non-digital form, e.g. paper, film (for analogue photographs or movies), magnetic tape (for analogue sound recordings or video recordings) or vinyl disc.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: analog storage medium

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal statement about something.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: announcement

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of selected literary or scholastics works, for example poems, short stories, plays or research papers.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: anthology

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A set of metadata elements, policies and guidelines defined for a particular application.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: application profile
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: The metadata elements used in the application profile may be drawn from more than one element sets, including locally defined sets.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A computer program, commonly abbreviated as 'API', that enables a separate computer to interact programmatically with the computer running it.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: API
application programming interface

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An archival document is a realization of the content related to an archival record. It can be exemplified as a book, a document, a letter, a database, etc.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: archival document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of archival document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: archival document set

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An archival record connotes a material created or received by a person, family, or organization, public or private, in the conduct of their affairs that is preserved because of the enduring value contained in the information it contains or as evidence of the function and the responsibilities of its creator.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: archival record

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of archival records.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: archival record set

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of a piece of writing on a particular topic, usually published within a periodical publication (e.g. journal, magazine and newspaper).
owl:Class.rdfs:label: article

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: It describes any work regarded as art in its widest sense, including works from literature and music, visual art, etc.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: artistic work

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of a sound recording.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: audio document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A controlled vocabulary or official list that establishes, for consistency, the authoritative forms of headings, and the preferred terms or proper names to be used, when creating a catalogue or when indexing and searching a set of entities within a defined domain.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: authority file

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A thesis reporting a research project undertaken as part of an undergraduate course of education leading to a bachelor's degree.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: bachelor's thesis

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A database providing an authoritative source of bibliographic information.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: bibliographic database
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Examples include PubMed (http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed), CrossRef Metadata Search (http://search.crossref.org/) and PubMed Central (http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/).

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Standard bibliographic metadata describing an expression of a work.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: bibliographic metadata
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: To take the example of a journal article, bibliographic metadata typically include the authors' names, the date of publication, the title of the article, the journal name and volume number, the first and last page numbers, and the Digital Object Identifier (DOI).

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An account of the events, works and achievements, both personal and professional, of a person, either living or dead.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: biography

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A Web publication medium containing blog posts.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: blog

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Information manifested in a blog, one of a set of periodic sequential entries containing commentary, descriptions of events, or other material such as images or videos, usually displayed in reverse-chronological order and usually maintained by an individual, or comments on such a post.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: blog post

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A non-serial document that is complete in one volume or a designated finite number of volumes. A book published by a publisher is usually identified by an International Standard Book Number (ISBN), and may be manifested as a physical printed publication on paper bound in a hard or soft cover, or in electronic format as an 'e-book'.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: book

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A defined chapter of a book, usually with a separate title or number.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: book chapter

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A written review and critical analysis of the content, scope and quality of a book or other monographic work.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: book review

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A sequence of books having certain characteristics in common that are formally identified together as a group.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: book series
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Examples include the books in the Law, Governance and Technology Series published by Springer.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A set of books having certain characteristics in common that informally allow their identification together as a group - for instance, the books of the Harry Potter saga.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: book set

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A brief report document. This term may also be used synonymously with Rapid Communication to mean 'A short rapidly published research article or conference paper, typically reporting significant research results that have been recently discovered, or a brief news item reporting such discoveries.'
owl:Class.rdfs:label: brief report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document published by a funding agency requesting submission of applications for financial grants to fund projects, for example to enable research investigations in areas specified in the Call.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: call for applications

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A part of a grant application that provides a description of a proposed project and gives reasons why it is worthy of funding.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: case for support

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document containing the case for support for a particular project, usually contained within a grant application document but sometimes distributed separately, without the financial and organizational information that the grant application document also contains.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: case for support document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A report about a particular case or situation.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: case report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A list of items describing the content of a resource, for example items in an exhibition, items offered for sale by a vendor, or entities contained within a library or collection. Ideally, catalogs are created according to specific and uniform principles of construction and are under the control of an authority file.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: catalog

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A defined document section, forming part of or intended for inclusion within a larger document, usually with its own title or chapter number. Different chapters within a document such as a book or a report may each be independently authored, or may all be authored by a single individual or group of authors.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: chapter

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Metadata describing the citations made within a work to other works, and (optionally) some characteristics of the expressions of the cited works.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: citation metadata

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A presentation of findings following a clinical or medical investigation on a human or animal patient, that may contain a diagnosis and proposals for therapeutic treatment and/or epidemiological control measures, or may propose further evaluative studies that will eventually lead to such outcomes.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: clinical case report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A recommendation on the appropriate treatment and care of people with a specific disease or condition, based on the best available evidence, designed to help healthcare professionals in their work.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: clinical guideline

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A predefined written procedural method, designed to ensure reliability of findings, for undertaking a medical or veterinary clinical study of the safety, efficacy, or optimum dosage schedule of one or more diagnostic, therapeutic or prophylactic drugs or treatments, or of devices or techniques, involving a randomized controlled trial for evidence-based assessment in humans or animals, specifying criteria of eligibility, nature of controls, sampling schedules, data collection parameters, statistical analyses, reporting standards, etc. to be employed in undertaking the clinical trial.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: clinical trial design

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The report of a pre-planned medical or veterinary clinical study of the safety, efficacy, or optimum dosage schedule of one or more diagnostic, therapeutic or prophylactic drugs, or of devices, treatments or techniques, involving a randomized controlled trial for evidence-based assessment in humans or animals selected according to predetermined criteria of eligibility and observed for evidence of favourable and unfavourable effects.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: clinical trial report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of the literary or scholastic works of a single person.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: collected works

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A verbal or written remark concerning some entity. In written form, a comment is often appended to that entity and termed an annotation. Within computer programs or ontologies, comments are added to enhance human understanding, and are usually prefaced by a special syntactic symbol that ensures they are ignored during execution of the program.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: comment

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of all the literary or scholastic works of a single person.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: complete works

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A computer program designed to assist a human user to perform one or more goal-oriented tasks such as word processing or image processing.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: computer application
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: A computer application will typically save its output files in one or more specific formats, conforming either to proprietary or open standards.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A digital item containing information in computer-readable form encoded in a particular format.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: computer file

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A unit of computer code in source or compiled form, employing one or more algorithms to be executed by a digital computer to undertake a particular task. Computer programs are collectively called 'software' to distinguish them from the equipment ('hardware') upon which they run.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: computer program

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A paper, typically the realization of a research paper reporting original research findings, usually published within a conference proceedings volume.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: conference paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A display poster, typically containing text with illustrative figures and/or tables, usually reporting research results or proposing hypotheses, submitted for acceptance to and/or presented at a conference, seminar, symposium, workshop or similar event.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: conference poster

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document containing the programme and collected conference papers, or their abstracts, presented at a conference, seminar, symposium or similar event.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: conference proceedings

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of selected words and phrases related to a particular domain of knowledge used to permit consistency of metadata annotation and improved retrieval following a search, in which homonyms, synonyms and similar ambiguities of meaning present in natural language are disambiguated.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: controlled vocabulary

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A correction to an error in a previously published document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: correction

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal correction to an error introduced by the author into a previously published document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: corrigendum

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A protective covering used to bind together the pages of a document or the first, informative, page of a digital document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: cover

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A new edition of a historical publication, edited by a scholar other than the original author, containing within the body text the supposedly best version of the original work, with footnotes detailing and commenting on textual variations between different versions, typically with an introduction to the original work written by the scholar, and with a bibliography listing related publications.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: critical edition

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A realisation of a fabio:Dataset (a frbr:Work) containing a defined collection of data with specific content and possibly with a specific version number, that can be embodied as a fabio:DigitalManifestation (a frbr:Manifestation with a specific format) and be represented by a specific fabio:ComputerFile (a frbr:Item) on someone's hard drive.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: data file

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A structured document giving information about how data arising from a research project or other endeavour is to be manages, preserved and shared.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: data management plan

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A policy that describes and defines how data should be managed, preserved and shared.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: data management policy

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document embodying a policy that describes and defines how data should be managed, preserved and shared.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: data management policy document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A repository for storing data.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: data repository

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A structured collection of logically related records or data usually stored and retrieved using computer-based means.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: database

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A software, commonly abbreviated as 'DBMS', used to create a database.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: database management system

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of related facts, often expressed in numerical form and encoded in a defined structure.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: dataset

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The final published expression of a work that bears the publisher's imprimatur.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: definitive version
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Typically for a journal article, the Definitive Version results from revision of an earlier submitted version of the work following peer review, and is then published in print and/or digital form after the publisher has assigned it a DOI. The Definitive Version is also known as the Version of Record, although according to the CrossRef Glossary (http://crossref.org/02publishers/glossary.html) that term can also refer to the author's final version of a work that is not formally published.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A demonstration paper, typically describing a new product, service or system created as a result of research, usually presented during a conference or workshop.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: demo paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A personal record, in a form of book, with discrete entries (often handwritten) arranged by date, reporting what has happened over the course of a day or other period of time.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: diary

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A digital object, such as a computer file.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: digital item
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: A digital item is an exemplar of a digital manifestation only and it is always stored in a storage medium suitable for digital objects, such as CDs, DVDs, HDs and the Web.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A manifestation that represents data in binary form, encoding the data as a series of 0s and 1s.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: digital manifestation

owl:Class.vann:example: fabio:floppy-disk a fabio:DigitalStorageMedium .
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A means of storing information in digital form, involving binary encoding of data in 0s and 1s, e.g. a computer random access memory, hard disc, USB stick, CD, DVD or digital magnetic tape.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: digital storage medium

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A database of information which is heavily optimized for reading.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: directory

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A discipline dictionary is a collection of subject disciplines.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: discipline dictionary

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A thesis reporting the research undertaken during a period of graduate study leading to a doctoral degree.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: doctoral thesis

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A repository for storing documents.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: document repository

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A detachable outer cover, usually made of paper and printed with text and illustrations. This outer cover has folded flaps that hold it to the cover of a document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: dust jacket

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of an opinion written by an editor.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: editorial

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A message transmitted over the internet as an item of electronic mail, typically based on the Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP). Emails can have computer files containing documents, dataset and images attached to them or embedded within them.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: e-mail

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Metadata describing the work itself, including for example the name of the creator(s), the title of the work, and the date and place of its creation.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: entity metadata

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An item written or printed in a diary, list, account book, reference book, or database.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: entry

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal correction to an error introduced by the publisher into a previously published document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: erratum

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A piece of non-fiction writing on a particular subject, usually of moderate length and without chapters.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: essay

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A set of questions on a particular topic designed to test the academic, professional or technical ability of the person taking the examination, with achievement of a pass grade in the examination typically being a prerequisite for the award of an educational award such as a degree, or of a professional or technical qualification.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: examination paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A segment or passage selected from a larger expression for use in another expression, usually with specific attribution to its original source.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: excerpt
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote:
An excerpt is more general than a quotation, and is generally used to indicate a re-published extract from a book, instruction manual, film, radio programme, etc, that need not be what someone said.
For example:
Oxford 01865 Oxshott 01372 Oxted 01883 Oxton 01578
is an excerpt from the UK Dialling Codes section of the Oxford Telephone Directory.
Similarly, the following concluding passage from William Wordsworth's poem Lines written a Few Miles above Tintern Abbey is an excerpt rather than a quotation:
Nor wilt thou then forget, That after many wanderings, many years Of absence, these steep woods and lofty cliffs, And this green pastoral landscape, were to me More dear, both for themselves and for thy sake.
Note: Use fabio:Excerpt to indicate a segment or passage selected from another expression that is not a passage of speech, and fabio:Quotation to indicate a segment or passage selected from another expression that is a passage of speech.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An executive summary is a brief report summarizing a longer formal report, designed to present the key points, conclusions and recommendations arising from the study being reported, for readers too busy to take the time to read the complete report.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: executive summary

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A predefined written procedural method, designed to ensure successful replication of results by others in the same or other laboratories, that describes the overall objectives, organization and implementation of a scientific experiment, and specifies the experimental design, experimental methods, reagents, instrumentation, sampling schedules, data collection parameters, statistical analyses, image processing procedures, safety precautions, reporting standards, etc. employed in undertaking the experiment.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: experimental protocol

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A subclass of FRBR Expression, restricted to expressions of `fabio:Work`.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: expression
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote:
For your latest research paper, the preprint submitted to the publisher, and the final published version to which the publisher assigned a unique digital object identifier, are both expressions of the same work.
A `fabio:Expression` can only have part or be part of another `fabio:Expression`. Moreover, it can be a representation only of a `fabio:Work`, and it can be embodied only in instances of `fabio:Manifestation`.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of expressions, for example a periodical or a book series.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: expression collection

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A visual communication object comprising one or more still images on a related theme. If included within a publication, a figure is typically unaligned with the main body of text, having its own descriptive textual figure legend.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: figure

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A movie with an accompanying soundtrack, typically created by a professional film studio, designed to communicate a fictional story, record an artistic event, or impart information that is scientific or documentary in nature.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: film

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A system of classification derived from the practice and method of collaboratively creating and managing tags to annotate and categorize content in a particular domain.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: folksonomy

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A horizontal bar chart used to guide project planning, execution and control, illustrating the project schedule, with a separate line indicating the start and end dates of each of the key project activities or workpackages, and optionally showing the dependencies between these items. A Gantt chart is typically part of a project plan.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: Gantt chart

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal written request for financial support from a grant-giving body in support of a project, for example an academic research project.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: grant application

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of a grant application, usually containing a case for support document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: grant application document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A print object bound with rigid protective covers (typically of cardboard covered with cloth, heavy paper, or sometimes leather).
owl:Class.rdfs:label: hardback

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A visual representation other than text, including all types of moving image and still image.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: image

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An 'In Brief' is a journal or magazine news item that describes all the articles (or all the important articles) in that issue of the periodical. The content of an 'In Brief' may be constructed from the abstracts of the articles it highlights, but is more likely to be written by a member of the periodical staff especially for the issue.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: in brief

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A scholarly work that describes applied and validated solutions such as software tools, systems or architectures that benefit from the use of the technology of a particular scholarly domain. Usually, papers of this kind should also provide convincing evidence that there is use of the proposed application or tool by the target user group, preferably outside the institution that conducted its development.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: in-use paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An alphabetically-ordered list of words and phrases ('headings') and associated pointers ('locators') to where useful material relating to that heading can be found in a document.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: index

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An instructional document typically supplied with a technologically advanced consumer product, such as a car or a computer application, or with an item of complex equipment such as a microscope.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: instruction manual

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A work created for the purpose of education or instruction, that may be expressed as a text book, a lecture, a tutorial or an instruction manual.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: instructional work

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A subclass of FRBR Item, restricted to exemplars of `fabio:Manifestation`.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: item
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote:
A `fabio:Item` can only have part or be part of another `fabio:Item`. Moreover, it can be an exemplar only of a `fabio:Manifestation`.
An example of a `fabio:Item` is a printed copy of a journal article on your desk, or a PDF file of that article that you purchased from a publisher and that now resides in digital form on your computer hard drive.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of items.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: item collection

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A scholarly periodical primarily devoted to the publication of original research papers.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: journal
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Printed and electronic manifestations of the same journal are usually identified by separate print and electronic International Standard Serial Numbers (ISSN or eISSN, respectively), that identifies the journal as a whole, not to individual issues of it.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An article, typically the realization of a research paper reporting original research findings, published in a journal issue.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: journal article

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An editorial published in an issue of a journal.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: journal editorial

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A particular published issue of a journal, one or more of which will constitute a volume of the journal.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: journal issue

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A news report published in a journal issue.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: journal news item

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A particular published volume of a journal, comprising one or more journal issues.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: journal volume

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A notebook used by an individual research scientist as the primary record of his or her research activities. A researcher may use a laboratory notebook to document hypotheses, to describe experiments and to record data in various formats, to provide details of data analysis and interpretation, or to record the validation or invalidation of the original hypotheses. The laboratory notebook serves as an organizational tool and a memory aid. It may also have a role in recording and protecting any intellectual property created during the research, and may be used in evidence when establishing priority of discoveries, for example in patent applications. Electronic versions of laboratory notebooks are increasingly being employed by researchers, particularly in chemistry and the pharmaceutical industry.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: laboratory notebook

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document containing notes that summarize a lecture or course of lectures.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: lecture notes

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A written explanation by a judge or group of judges that accompanies a ruling in a legal case, laying out the reasons and legal principles for the ruling, and sometimes containing pronouncements about what the law is and how it should be interpreted.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: legal opinion

owl:Class.vann:example: :my-letter a fabio:Letter ; dcterms:description 'A written message addressed to a person or organization, typically sent by post or messenger.'@en .
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A written or printed communication of a personal or professional nature between individuals and/or representatives of corporate bodies, usually transmitted by the postal service or published in a periodical. In the latter case, the letter is typically addressed to the editor and comments on or discussed an item previously published by that periodical, or of interest to its readership.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: letter

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The catalog of the holdings of a library, for example that of the Library of Congress (http://catalog.loc.gov/).
owl:Class.rdfs:label: library catalog

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A literary creative work, such as a novel, play, poem or song.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: literary artistic work

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A periodical, usually devoted to a particular topic or domain of interest, and usually published weekly or monthly, consisting primarily of non-peer reviewed editorials, journalistic news items and more substantive articles, reviews, book reviews and discussions concerning current or recent events and publications, and matters of interest to the domain served by the magazine.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: magazine
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Some scientific journals, notably Science and Nature, also secondarily serve as science magazines by containing substantive editorials and news items on vital or controversial issues.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An article published in a magazine issue.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: magazine article

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An editorial published in an issue of a magazine.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: magazine editorial

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A particular published issue of a magazine, identified by date, and sometimes also by place (e.g. 'West Coast edition') or language (e.g. 'Spanish edition').
owl:Class.rdfs:label: magazine issue

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A news report published in a magazine issue.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: magazine news item

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A subclass of FRBR Manifestation, restricted to manifestations of `fabio:Expression`. `fabio:Manifestation` specifically applies to electronic (digital) as well as to physical manifestations of expressions.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: manifestation
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote:
A `fabio:Manifestation` can only have part or be part of another `fabio:Manifestation`. Moreover, it can be an embodiment only of a `fabio:Expression` and it can be exemplified only by instances of `fabio:Item`.
Examples of different manifestations of a single 'version of record' expression of a scholarly work include an article in a print journal or the on-line version of that article as a web page.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A collection of manifestations.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: manifestation collection

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A textual work prepared 'by hand', such as a typescript or word-processed pre-publication draft of a research paper or a report, or a work not otherwise reproduced in multiple copies.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: manuscript
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Note: `fabio:Manuscript` is not intended to describe a handwritten historical document on paper or parchment, for which the FRBR distinctions between work, expression, manifestation and item (individual copy) becomes blurred.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A thesis reporting a research project undertaken as part of a graduate course of education leading to a master's degree.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: master's thesis

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A report of a meeting of some kind.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: meeting report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A separate work that provides information describing one or more characteristics of a resource or entity.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: metadata

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document that contains metadata information describing one or more characteristics of an entity.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: metadata document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A scholarly work detailing a method, procedure or experimental protocol employed in a particular scholarly domain.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: methods paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A social networking publication medium such as Twitter, Tumblr, FriendFeed, Facebook or MySpace. A microblog differs from a traditional blog in that its individual content items are smaller than a traditional blog posts, typically containing just a short sentence, a single image, or a URI. These small messages are referred to as microposts.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: microblog

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A content item that is published in a Microblog, typically containing just a short sentence, a single image, or a URL.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: micropost

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A metadata standard specifying items to be included when creating metadata describing a dataset of a particular type, or when creating a structured summary of the main findings of an article or report in a particular domain of interest, thereby ensuring adequate descriptive information is recorded for subsequent resource discovery and/or interpretation of the information described.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: minimal information standard

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A mathematical, graphical or physical representation of some physical reality, conceptual idea or theoretical construct.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: model

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of a moving image.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: movie

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A moving display, either generated dynamically by a computer program or formed from a series of pre-recorded still images imparting an impression of motion when shown in succession.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: moving image
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Examples include animations, cine films, videos, and computational simulations. Expressions of moving images may incorporate synchronized soundtracks.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A piece of music, typically in the form of a composition recorded in musical notation.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: musical composition

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A single, attributable and machine-readable factual assertion - the smallest unit of publishable information that can be uniquely identified and attributed to its author - typically expressed in RDF.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: nanopublication

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A published news report.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: news item

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A report of an item of news.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: news report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A non-peer reviewed periodical, usually published daily or weekly, consisting primarily of editorials and news items concerning current or recent events and matters of public interest.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: newspaper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An article written by a journalist and published in a newspaper.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: newspaper article

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An editorial published in an issue of a newspaper.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: newspaper editorial

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A particular published issue of a newspaper, identified by date, and sometimes also by place or time (e.g. 'Late London Edition').
owl:Class.rdfs:label: newspaper issue

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A news report published in a newspaper issue.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: newspaper news item

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A book containing personal notes, typically created by writing into a physical book with blank pages.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: notebook

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A notification of receipt of something, for example of receipt of a book that will later be the subject of a book review.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: notification of receipt

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A long fictitious narrative written in literary prose.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: novel

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A news item reporting the death of a person, typically accompanied by an description of that person's life and contributions to his or her profession and to society at large.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: obituary

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal representation of a set of concepts within a domain of knowledge, and the logical relationships between those concepts.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: ontology

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document containing an ontology.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: ontology document
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Examples include an OWL (Web Ontology Language) file (http://www.w3.org/TR/owl-features/).

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An expression of a personal or professional opinion on an issue or topic.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: opinion

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal speech, for example one delivered at a ceremonial occasion, or the written transcript of such a speech.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: oration

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A manifestation that represents pages either in physical (e.g., one side of a sheet of paper) or in digital form (e.g., a page in a PDF, or a web page).
owl:Class.rdfs:label: page

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A print object with a flexible cover, usually made of paper or paperboard.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: paperback

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal disclosure of a new invention approved by a governmental patent agency, made to register intellectual property rights, and to give exclusive rights to the inventor or assignee to manufacture, use, license or sell the invention for a certain number of years.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: patent

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal disclosure of a new invention, made in application for a patent.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: patent application

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The physical or electronic realization of a patent application.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: patent application document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The physical or electronic realization of a patent.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: patent document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A publication issued on a regular and ongoing basis as a series of issues, each issue comprising separate periodical items, for example editorials, articles, news items and/or other writings.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: periodical

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A particular issue of a periodical, identified and distinguished from other issues of the same publication by date and/or issue number and/or volume number, and comprising separate periodical items such as editorials, articles and news items.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: periodical issue

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A piece of writing published in a periodical issue, typically accompanied by other items by different authors.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: periodical item

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A particular published volume of a periodical.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: periodical volume

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Information communicated personally by verbal or written means from one individual to one or more another persons or organizations.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: personal communication

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A paper, usually presented during a specific session of a conference dedicated to Ph.D. students, that describes ongoing Ph.D. student's research.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ph.D. symposium paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A form of literature written by a playwright, usually consisting of scripted dialogue between characters, intended for theatrical performance rather than reading.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: play

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An artistic work written with an intensity or beauty of language more characteristic of poetry than of prose.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: poem

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A description and definition of how something should be done. Ideally, a policy should be both effective in achieving its goals and acceptable to those who have to abide by it.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: policy

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document embodying a policy that describes and defines how something should be done.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: policy document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A scholarly work that reports a particular intellectual position or viewpoint regarding a particular scholarly topic. Usually, these papers are dependent on the author's opinion or interpretation, do not have an evaluation, and need to present relevant and novel discussion points in a thorough manner.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: position paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A paper that typically accompanies a poster describing some preliminary results of research, usually presented during a conference or a workshop.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: poster paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The version of an author's original scholarly work, such as a research paper or a review, re-submitted for publication after revision by the author in the light of comments from reviewers.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: postprint
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Note: For the version before peer review, use fabio:Preprint. For the final published version, use fabio:DefinitiveVersion.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The version of an author's original scholarly work, such as a research paper or a review, first submitted to publisher for publication.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: preprint
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Note: For that version resubmitted after peer-review and revision, use fabio:Postprint. For the final published version use fabio:DefinitiveVersion.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A set of slides containing text, tables or figures, designed to communicate ideas or research results, for projection and viewing by an audience at a conference, symposium, seminar, lecture, workshop or other gatherings, typically embodied in a particular manifestation format such as a SlideShare or PowerPoint slideshow.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: presentation

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A news report published by an organization to provide information to journalists.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: press release

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An analog manifestation in physical printed form, typically on paper.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: print object

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A paper, typically the realization of a research paper reporting original research findings, usually published within an academic proceedings volume.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: proceedings paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A written review and critical analysis of the purpose, features, performance and other qualities of a product.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: product review

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Metadata describing a project.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: project metadata
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Examples include the project name, the names of those who conducted the project, the name of the institution in which the project was conducted, and the project funding information.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document used to guide project planning, execution and control, specifying the project's goal and objectives and the activities and resources required to achieve these, setting out the project schedule, and identifying the major workpackages, milestones and deliverables. A project plan will typically contain a Gantt chart.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: project plan

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A report describing the outcomes of specific project, typically listing 'deliverables' created or 'milestones' achieved during the project.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: deliverable report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A document containing a project report, intended to be delivered to a customer or funding agency describing the results achieved within a specific project.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: deliverable

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: In printing and publishing, a proof copy is the preliminary version of a publication, after the inclusion of any author corrections following review, and after copy editing and formatting to bring the manuscript into the house style, intended for final checking prior to publication to detect and eliminate typographical errors, omissions or transpositions of text, incorrect layout or placement of illustrations and tables, or other formatting errors. Those who check proofs include the editor, possibly the peer-reviewers (to ensure that their requested modifications have been included to their satisfaction), possibly an in-house professional proof-reader, and / or the author, who is ultimately responsible for ensuring the published work says what (s)he means it to say. Substantive changes to the text are not permitted once the manuscript has reached proof stage.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: proof

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A proposal or proposition of a new conceptualization, hypothesis, idea, theory, activity or organisation.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: proposition

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A set of questions on a particular topic, usually in the form of multiple choice questions requiring the respondent to select the correct answer, or providing the ability to indicate support for or against a proposal on a numerical scale, designed for rapid numerical analysis of responses and often used in surveying public opinion.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: questionnaire

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A passage of speech selected from a larger verbal or written expression for use in another expression, with specific attribution to its original source, and usually demarcated by quotation marks and / or by placing it in a separate indented paragraph.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: quotation
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote:
A quotation is a repetition of what someone has said, and is presented 'within quotation marks', for example:
On June 4th 1940, Winston Churchill made a speech on the radio that has since become famous, that included the words: ' . . . we shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall fight in the fields and in the streets, we shall fight in the hills; we shall never surrender . . .'
Similarly, the words 'but Brutus is an honourable man' from Mark Antony's funeral speech in Shakespeare's play Julius Caesar is a quotation, since Mark Antony says these words in the play.
Note: Use fabio:Quotation to indicate a segment or passage selected from another expression that is a passage of speech, and fabio:Excerpt to indicate a segment or passage selected from another expression that is not a passage of speech.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A short rapidly published research article or conference paper, typically reporting significant research results that have been recently discovered, or a brief news item reporting such discoveries.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: rapid communication

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A book containing authoritative factual information, such as a dictionary, encyclopaedia, handbook or field guide, which is a realisation of a certain reference work and may contain several reference entries.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: reference book

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A particular reference entry containing authoritative factual information on a certain topic, usually contained in a larger expression.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: reference entry

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A work to which people refer for authoritative factual information, such as a dictionary, encyclopaedia, entry, handbook or field guide, or an informative web page such as an institutional, research group or project home page.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: reference work

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A database in which the data are arranged in tables according to their common characteristics, with relationships between the tables being defined by a relational model or schema. A relational database is highly optimized for performance, and is queried using a database query language such as SQL (Structured Query Language). The software used to create a relational database is called a relational database management system (RDBMS).
owl:Class.rdfs:label: relational database

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A work that is a reply, either to a letter or other direct communication, or to feedback or comments about a piece of submitted writing. The latter is typically written by the author of a journal article submitted for publication, or by an applicant making a grant application, in response to reviews of the work from peer reviewers prior to publication (for the journal article) or prior to funding decision (for the grant application). Alternatively, it can be written in response to post-publication peer-review of a published journal article, or comments about it.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: reply

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A formal factual, methodological, statistical, technical or research report issued by an individual, group, agency, government body or other institution.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: report

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of a report, usually in printed form.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: report document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A set of recommendations for the minimum reporting requirements to be employed when reporting a particular type of investigation or project, for example a randomized clinical trial. A reporting standard may involve a checklist and a flow diagram, offers a standard way for authors to prepare a complete and transparent report of their findings, and aids their critical appraisal and interpretation of their data.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: reporting standard

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A computer system in which information may be stored.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: repository

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A scholarly work that reports original research contributions addressing theoretical, analytical or experimental aspects of a particular scholarly domain.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: research paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A scholarly work that describes resources developed to provide experimental materials or facilities, support a research hypothesis, to provide answers to a research question, or that have contributed to the generation of novel scientific work.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: resource paper
owl:Class.skos:scopeNote: Examples of such resources include, for experimental sciences, mouse mutant lines and large communally used X-ray or neutron sources, and, for computer sciences, datasets, ontologies, vocabularies, ontology design patterns, evaluation benchmarks or methods, services, APIs and software frameworks, workflows, crowdsourcing task designs, protocols and metrics.

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A public statement made about an earlier statement that withdraws, cancels, refutes, diametrically reverses the original statement or ceases and desists from publishing the original statement.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: retraction

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of a retraction. A formal document which publicly withdraws, cancels, refutes, reverses an earlier statement or publication or announces the act of desisting from publishing the original statement.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: retraction notice

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A review of others' work.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: review

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An article that contains a review.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: review article

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A scholarly work that surveys the state of the art of topics central to a particular subject or relating to a specific domain (e.g. the scope of a certain journal or conference). Papers of this kind may contain a selective bibliography listing key papers related to the subject or providing advice on information sources, or they may strive to be comprehensive, covering all contributions to the development of a topic and exploring their different findings or views.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: review paper

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A work that reports scholarly activity on a particular topic, either published in written form, or delivered orally at a meeting.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: scholarly work

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A written work made especially for a film or television program. Screenplays can be original works or adaptations from existing pieces of writing, for example novels.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: screenplay

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A small computer program written in a scripting language such as JavaScript, PHP or Perl that allows control of one or more software applications.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: script

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A sequence of expressions having certain characteristics in common that are formally identified together as a group.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: series

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A work of fiction that is usually written in prose, often in narrative format. This format tends to be more focused and less elaborate than longer works of fiction such as novels.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: short story

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The distinct intellectual creation of a computer program or software application. It represents the abstract concept, logic, and intended functionality of the software, independent of any specific source code implementation, programming language, or compiled version.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: software

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A musical composition that contains vocal parts ('lyrics') that are performed ('sung').
owl:Class.rdfs:label: song

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The creative work of making an electrical or mechanical recording of sounds, such as the spoken voice, singing, instrumental music, animal vocalizations or sound effects.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: sound recording

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An explicit description of, or set of requirements to be satisfied by, a material, product, resource, service or standard.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: specification

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The realization of a specification (a standard, a workflow, etc.).
owl:Class.rdfs:label: specification document

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An electronic form of data storage that displays a grid of rows and columns, in which each editable cell can contain alphanumeric text, a numeric value, or a formula that defines how the content of that cell is to be calculated from the content of another cell or cells.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: spreadsheet

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Clear and detailed written instructions of a prescribed step-by-step procedure to be routinely followed, and decisions to be made when undertaking a specific task, process or function, to achieve consistent performance, ensure safety and/or assure data quality.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: standard operating procedure

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A recorded static visual representation. This class of image includes diagrams, drawings, graphs, graphic designs, plans, maps, photographs and prints.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: still image

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A device for recording information or storing data.
owl:Class.rdfs:label: storage medium

owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A structured summary containing essential metadata describing a research investigation and/or the research outputs that have resulted from it, for example datasets and journal articles, structured according to some minimal information standard.
